Japanese: ６月２１日　午前１１時３７分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: June 21, 11:37 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: あ‥‥あぶなかった。
English: Phew, that was a close one.

Japanese: なるほどくんの弁護は、 被告席で聞くもんじゃないね。
English: I know you were giving it your all to defend me.

Japanese: もう、かぎりなく 死刑の気分だったよ。
English: But I could feel the death penalty hanging in the air...

Japanese: ぼくも、 ほとんど死にかけてた。
English: I felt like I was pretty close to dying myself...

Japanese: でも、やっぱり なるほどくんはすごいです。
English: But you were wonderful out there.

Japanese: 耳を疑うようなヘ理屈をこねて、 みなさんをたぶらかすのですから。
English: You listened carefully, made theories, and tricked everyone into believing you.

Japanese: ‥‥あ、ありがとう。
English: Th-Thanks. Wait... Tricked??

Japanese: あれが、ベンゴシの使う 秘術なのですね‥‥。
English: That must be the "lawyer's" secret technique, right?

Japanese: あのさ‥‥。 次に出てくる証人って、やっぱり？
English: By the way... Is the next witness who I think it is...?

Japanese: ナツミさんだよ。
English: Yeah, I'm pretty sure it's Lotta Hart.

Japanese: うう‥‥あいかわらずヒドい。
English: Oh... Another jam, like always...

Japanese: ヤクソクだよ、なるほどくん！ きっと助けてね！
English: Remember, Nick! You promised you'd save me!

Japanese: 負けたら、お姉ちゃんといっしょに バケて出ちゃうから！
English: If you lose, my ghost will come after you with my sis', and we'll haunt you!

Japanese: おねえさま‥‥？ あの。千尋さまのことですか？
English: Your sister...? Um, are you talking about Mystic Mia?

Japanese: うん。はみちゃん、 知らなかったっけ？
English: Yeah. Do you remember her, Pearly?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんね、エラい 弁護士さんだったんだよ。
English: My sister was a super good lawyer!

Japanese: なるほどくんの、お師匠さん。
English: She was also Nick's teacher.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったのですか‥‥。
English: ...Oh, I didn't know that...

Japanese: 同日　午前１１時４３分 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: June 21, 11:43 AM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: それでは、審理を再開します。 本日の予定では‥‥
English: Court will now reconvene. Now, the first order of...

Japanese: くほっ！
English: Oww!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトは どうでもよろしい。
English: I don't want to hear it, old man.

Japanese: 証人を呼ぶから、 ちょっとダマってなさいな。
English: After I call my witness, sit there quietly and watch like a good little boy.

Japanese: は。
English: Y-Yes, sir.

Japanese: 事件当日、霊媒に立ち会っていた カメラマンをここへ！
English: Bring in the photographer who witnessed the channeling!

Japanese: 証人。名前と職業を。
English: Witness. Your name and occupation, if you please.

Japanese: よっ、ナルホドー。 ひさしぶりィ！
English: Hey, Phoenix! How ya doin'?

Japanese: どうも‥‥
English: Just fine...

Japanese: ゴメンなぁ。 ウチ、ついチョーシに‥‥
English: Sorry 'bout this. Didn't exactly have a choice, ya know...?

Japanese: ぎゃあっ！
English: Yeowza!

Japanese: ‥‥名前と職業。
English: Name and occupation.

Japanese: ななな、ナニするんやッ！
English: I-I declare! What in tarnation!?

Japanese: 裁判長コラ！ ボーリョクやでコレは！
English: Hey, Judge! This here is violence against my fair self!

Japanese: いいんです。
English: That's fine.

Japanese: いいコトあるかいッ！ ‥‥それも、ムチやでムチ！
English: "That's fine!?" That ain't fine!! That's a whip!

Japanese: ぎええっ！
English: Eaaah!

Japanese: バカのバカさわぎほど バカげたものはない‥‥。
English: There's no need for foolish outcries from foolishly foolish fools.

Japanese: さっさと事件があった日の ことを証言なさい。
English: Just hurry up and testify about what happened on the day of the murder.

Japanese: なんや、まだ言うてへんで、 名前と職業‥‥。
English: I haven't even gotten to say my name and job yet...

Japanese: カメラマン・大沢木　ナツミ、 証言させてもらいマス！
English: The name's Lotta Hart, paranormal photographer, and I'm here to testify!

Japanese: もっとなごやかに やりましょうよ‥‥
English: Now, now, let's all be one big happy family... OK...?

Japanese: ～事件当日について～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: 《対面の間》には、被告人と 霧崎センセだけが入ったんや。
English: Only the Doc and the defendant went into the Channelin' Chamber.

Japanese: で、扉の前で待っとったら、 バアン！　銃声が聞こえたワケや！
English: We were waitin' outside the door and then, "bang!" we hear this gunshot!

Japanese: ナルホドーが扉をコワして、 いっしょに現場に踏みこむと！
English: Mr. Lawyer there broke the door down, and we rushed into the room.

Japanese: 殺された被害者と、ピストルを ぶらさげた被告がいたワケや。
English: Inside was the dead victim, and the defendant, wavin' a pistol around.

Japanese: 部屋の中には、その２人しか おらんかったで！
English: I swear, other than those two, there was no one else in the room.

Japanese: 事件直後の写真は 撮ったのですか？
English: And did you take a picture of the scene right after the murder took place?

Japanese: メチャロンやッ！
English: Reckon course!

Japanese: え。‥‥なんですか？
English: Eh? ...Excuse me?

Japanese: メチャロン。 “メチャ、もちロン”。
English: Reckon course! It's short for "Ya reckon!? Of course I did!"

Japanese: あぎゃあ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: そんなコトは どうでもよろしい。
English: Does it look like I care?

Japanese: ‥‥さっさと その写真を提出なさい。
English: Just hurry up and show your picture to the court.

Japanese: （いよいよだ‥‥。 どんな写真なんだ‥‥？）
English: (The moment of truth. What will be in that picture?)

Japanese: えーとな。 ‥‥ホラ、コレや！
English: Umm, let's see here... This here's the picture!
Hmm...

Japanese: たしかに、被害者と被告の他には、 だれもいませんな‥‥。
English: It would certainly seem that only the defendant and the victim were in that room.

Japanese: 証拠品《ナツミの写真》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Lotta's Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: いかがかしら‥‥？ 成歩堂　龍一。
English: Are you ready, Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: （ギモンはすべて 晴らしてやるさ‥‥）
English: (I will clear all doubt about Maya through this cross-examination...)

Japanese: （‥‥尋問でな！）
English: (Just watch me!)

Japanese: ～事件当日について～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: 《対面の間》には、被告人と 霧崎センセだけが入ったんや。
English: Only the Doc and the defendant went into the Channelin' Chamber.

Japanese: で、扉の前で待っとったら、 バアン！　銃声が聞こえたワケや！
English: We were waitin' outside the door and then, "bang!" we hear this gunshot!

Japanese: ナルホドーが扉をコワして、 いっしょに現場に踏みこむと！
English: Mr. Lawyer there broke the door down, and we rushed into the room.

Japanese: 殺された被害者と、ピストルを ぶらさげた被告がいたワケや。
English: Inside was the dead victim, and the defendant, wavin' a pistol around.

Japanese: 部屋の中には、その２人しか おらんかったで！
English: I swear, other than those two, there was no one else in the room.

Japanese: （くそっ！　‥‥どこにも つけいるスキがない‥‥）
English: (Argh! Her story adds up...)

Japanese: （そもそも、あのときは ぼくも現場にいたんだ）
English: (What's worse is that I was there at the scene of the crime at that time too,)

Japanese: （この証言にウソがないことは、 よくわかってるんだよな‥‥）
English: (and her story is exactly how I would have told it...)

Japanese: 本当に２人だけだったんですか！
English: Was it really only those two that went in!?

Japanese: ナニ言ってるの！ 弁護人もその場にいたはずでしょ！
English: What are you blabbering about!? You were there too, were you not!?

Japanese: う。
English: Urk.

Japanese: 自分で答えなさい！ 本当に２人だけだったの？
English: Why don't you answer that yourself? Was it really just the two of them?

Japanese: ‥‥ハイ。霧崎先生と真宵ちゃん だけでした‥‥。
English: ...Yes. Dr. Grey and Maya were the only two who went into the room...

Japanese: こ、こらこら。弁護人が 証言してどうするんですか！
English: H-Hold on! We can't have the defense testifying against its own client!

Japanese: とにかく。《対面の間》に入った のは、２人だけだったワケや。
English: Anyway, folks, only the two of 'em went into the Channeling Chamber, ya hear?

Japanese: 最初からわかりきったことね。 ‥‥証言をつづけなさい。
English: But you knew that from the very beginning, didn't you? ...Witness, continue.

Japanese: 本当に銃声だったんですか！
English: Was it really a gunshot!?

Japanese: ナニを言いだすの！ 自分で聞いているはずよ！
English: Are you insane!? You must have heard it as well!

Japanese: え。
English: Urk.

Japanese: ついでだから証言なさい。 本当に銃声だったの？
English: Why don't you testify for the court, Mr. Phoenix Wright? Was it really a gunshot?

Japanese: ‥‥ハイ。銃声のような 音だったと思います‥‥。
English: ...Yes. I think it was something that sounded like a gunshot...

Japanese: だ、だからどうして弁護人が 証言するんですかッ！
English: And why are we here listening to the defense testify!?

Japanese: （‥‥やりにくいな‥‥）
English: (...And the world becomes just a little crueler...)

Japanese: とにかく、あれは銃声や。 ムカシ、聞いたコトあるし。
English: I heard a gunshot a long time ago, so I know. And I tell ya that was a gunshot I heard.

Japanese: でな。でな。 ここから盛り上がるんや！
English: Now, this -- this is where the story heats up!

Japanese: ナルホドーが扉をコワした！ まちがいありませんか！
English: So, let me get this straight! This "Mr. Lawyer there" broke the door down!?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 自分で言っててムナしくないの？
English: Honestly, Mr. Phoenix Wright. Why don't you tell us what happened, then?

Japanese: うう‥‥ボクがコワしました。 ゴメンナサイ。
English: Um... Yes, Ms. von Karma... I broke the door down. Sorry.

Japanese: （小学生みたいな気分に なってきた‥‥）
English: (Why does this feel like an inquisition?)

Japanese: いやー。 あれはグッと来たでえ。
English: No need for apologizin'! That was great! You're a real man!

Japanese: ‥‥ほほお。 それは見たかったですな。
English: Oh, really? That's something I would've liked to see.

Japanese: （裁判長、ノンキに笑ってる‥‥）
English: (The judge is smiling rather openly. I'll take that as a good sign...)

Japanese: それで‥‥ 証人が現場で見たものは？
English: And? What did you see once you broke in?

Japanese: まちがいなく 被告人・綾里　真宵でしたか！
English: Are you sure it was the defendant, Maya Fey!?

Japanese: そうや。あの子やったわ。
English: Sure, I'm sure!

Japanese: （ナツミさん、 自信マンマンだな‥‥）
English: (Lotta seems awful confident in her testimony...)

Japanese: ナツミさん！ よく思い出してください。
English: Lotta! Please, think back to that day one more time.

Japanese: な‥‥なんや？
English: Wh-Whatcha getting' at?

Japanese: 現場に踏みこんだとき、 なんて言いましたっけ‥‥？
English: Remember what you said when we broke into the room...?

Japanese: あたしは‥‥殺されたの‥‥
English: ...I was... murdered...
*klik*

Japanese: ナツミさん！ ‥‥こんなときに‥‥
English: Lotta! At a time like this!?

Japanese: こんなときだからこそ、や！
English: Times like this are perfect for snapping up shots!

Japanese: それより、ど‥‥どういうことや！ この子‥‥真宵はん‥‥なんか？
English: But anyway... what's going on here!? This gal... Is she... ...Maya!?

Japanese: あなたは犯人を見たとき、 彼女が綾里　真宵かどうか‥‥
English: When you saw the murderer at that time,

Japanese: ハッキリわからなかった じゃないですか！
English: you couldn't even tell if it was Maya Fey or not! Am I correct!?

Japanese: あ、あれ！　そういえば‥‥。
English: Ah, um, well, ya see...

Japanese: 静粛に！　‥‥証人。 イイカゲンな発言は困ります！
English: Order! Ms. Hart! You are here to present accurate testimony!

Japanese: いや、ゴ‥‥ゴメンな。
English: Yeah, ah, s-sorry.

Japanese: （よし、これで状況は 変わるはずだ‥‥）
English: (Good. This shifts things back to my side...)

Japanese: アマいわ、成歩堂　龍一‥‥。
English: For a defense lawyer, your defense is terribly lacking, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 彼らが《対面の間》に入った ときのことを思い出すの、証人。
English: Witness. Think back to when those two entered the Channeling Chamber.

Japanese: “入ったとき”‥‥？
English: When they entered...?

Japanese: 《対面の間》に入ったのは、 綾里　真宵だったんでしょう？
English: One of those people that entered the chamber was Maya Fey, correct?

Japanese: あっ、そうや！
English: Yeah, that's right!

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに‥‥ あれは真宵ちゃんだったな‥‥）
English: (Yeah... It was Maya that went in.)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: どうやら結論は出たようですね。
English: It looks like we've come to a collective conclusion.

Japanese: この写真の人物は、やはり 綾里　真宵ということです。
English: The person in this picture is most certainly Maya Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: （‥‥また１つ、逃げ道を ふさがれたか‥‥）
English: (Rats! And I'm trapped like one... Again!)

Japanese: （つっこむも何も、あれが だれだったか、ぼくも見ている）
English: (Even I can see that any normal person would know who that was.)

Japanese: （‥‥ムダだ。 やめておこう‥‥）
English: (It's no good. I shouldn't push my luck on this...)

Japanese: まちがいありませんか！
English: Are you absolutely certain of that!?

Japanese: あらへん！　あのオバハンに 部屋から追い払われたケド‥‥、
English: Sure am! But that old witch chased us out of the room, so...

Japanese: （オバハン‥‥キミ子さんか）
English: ("Old witch"? Does she mean Morgan Fey?)

Japanese: あの部屋には ダレもおらんかったで！
English: But there was no one else in there, and you know it!

Japanese: （対面の間は、かなり うす暗かったはずだ‥‥）
English: (The Channeling Chamber was very dark, almost pitch black...)

Japanese: （本当にだれもいなかったのか？）
English: (Was there really no one else in there? What about...?)

Japanese: たとえば‥‥ビョウブのウラは？ 見ましたか？
English: What about... behind the folding screen? Did you check back there?

Japanese: メチャリマエやッ！
English: Reckgiven!

Japanese: え。‥‥なんですか？
English: Huh? What in the...?

Japanese: メチャリマエ。 “メチャ、あたりマエ”。
English: Reckgiven! Ya know! "Ya reckon!? That's a given!"

Japanese: いちおう、部屋の中は ざっと見たんや！
English: I took me a good look around the room once!

Japanese: ビョウブのウラ、ヒトなんか おらんかったハズやで！
English: There ain't no way anyone was hangin' out behind that foldin' screen!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥証人。 ちょっといいですか？
English: Hmm... If I may have a word with you, Ms. Hart...

Japanese: な、なんや。 モンクでもあるんかッ！
English: Wh-What? Ya got a problem!?

Japanese: メチャリマエ‥‥は、ちょっと イミがわかりにくいですな。
English: Reckgiven!... I mean, I am having a little difficulty in understanding you.

Japanese: むしろ、メタリマエ、のほうが より伝わりやす
English: If you would stop saying such things as "Reckongiven" and so forth...

Japanese: あひゃ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: さあ、どうかしら成歩堂　龍一。 ムジュンは見つかったかしら？
English: Now then, Mr. Phoenix Wright, do you see a problem with the testimony or don't you?

Japanese: たとえば‥‥扉のかげは？ 見ましたか？
English: What about... behind the door? Did you check there?

Japanese: ナメとんのかアンタ！
English: What do I look like to you? I wasn't born yesterday, ya know!

Japanese: だいいち、あの扉を開けたのは アンタやろ！
English: First off, the one that bust that door open was you!

Japanese: あんなイキオイよく “バーン”てけとばしたら、
English: With the way ya slammed that door,

Japanese: ドアのかげにいたヤツ、 鼻血だらだらやッ！
English: anyone standin' behind it woulda had more face and less nose, free of charge!

Japanese: ちなみに、扉のウラから 鼻血は検出されていない。
English: In any case, there was no one standing behind the door.

Japanese: （わかってるよ！　そんなこと）
English: (She actually had the police investigate that too!?)

Japanese: たとえば‥‥タタミの下は？ 見ましたか？
English: What about... under the flooring? Did you check there?

Japanese: メチャクリこかしたろかィ！
English: I ain't no ignoramus to make it out here!

Japanese: は。‥‥なんですか？
English: Huh?... Wh-What are you talking about?

Japanese: “なんですか”やないッ！
English: Don't "what are ya talkin' about" me!

Japanese: タタミの下ァ？ ‥‥ペッタンコにしたろか！
English: Under the floorin'? C'mon!, even a bug wouldn't crawl under that!

Japanese: ちなみに、タタミの下には ヒミツの抜け道は、なかった。
English: Sorry to disappoint, but there was no secret passageway under the flooring.

Japanese: （そりゃそうだろうな‥‥）
English: (Oh... Yeah, that was a bit far-fetched, huh...?)

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり、ダメだ！ ナツミさんの証言にウソはない）
English: (Argh! Just as I thought, there is nothing wrong with her testimony!)

Japanese: （それは、いっしょにいたぼくが 一番よく知っている‥‥）
English: (I was with her, so I know she's telling the truth...)

Japanese: もう、けっこうです。
English: Enough.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、証言には まったく問題ないようですね。
English: It seems that there are no issues with Ms. Hart's testimony.

Japanese: 事件が起こった瞬間、現場には ２人の人間しかいなかった。
English: At the time of the crime, only two people were in the Channeling Chamber:

Japanese: 被害者である霧崎　哲郎と、 被告人・綾里　真宵。
English: the victim, Dr. Turner Grey, and the defendant, Maya Fey.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: この写真では、被告のカオは ハッキリわかりませんが‥‥、
English: The face of the defendant can not be verified in this picture.

Japanese: しかし状況から考えて、 被告人と断定していいようですね。
English: However, if we think about the circumstances, it would have to be the defendant.

Japanese: （‥‥どうすればいいんだ！ このままじゃあ‥‥）
English: (What am I going to do!? If I just let this go...)

Japanese: どうですか？　弁護人。 何かありますか‥‥？
English: What is it, Mr. Wright? You look as if you have something to say...

Japanese: （‥‥ヘタに答えると 裁判長の心証を悪くするな‥‥）
English: (If I make one wrong move, I'm going to have the judge against me...)

Japanese: （“写ってるのが真宵ちゃん じゃない”と証明できるか？）
English: (Is there anything I can present that would prove it's not Maya in that photo?)

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: な、なんですか、 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥この写真に写っているのは、 被告ではないかもしれません！
English: It is possible that the person in this photo is not the defendant!

Japanese: な、な、な‥‥、 なんですってぇぇっ！
English: Wh-Wh-What!? Do you know what you are proposing!?

Japanese: そ、そそ、そ‥‥、 そんなバカなぁぁっ！
English: Y-Y-Yeah! That's just plain fool's talk!

Japanese: （そこまでおどろかれると、 自信なくなるなあ‥‥）
English: (Thanks guys. Your show of support was just great...)

Japanese: ‥‥では、証拠を見せて もらいましょう。
English: ...Now, then, Mr. Wright. Please show the court some proof.

Japanese: 写真の人物が、綾里　真宵 ではない、という証拠を！
English: Prove to this court that the person in this photo is not Maya Fey!

Japanese: その証拠は、これです！
English: Here is your proof!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: Is there a problem?

Japanese: “それはちがうでしょ” という気持ちを‥‥、
English: Oh, no. I thought I would work on my inflections.

Japanese: “異議あり”のニュアンスで 表現してみたわ。
English: Just wondering what an "Objection!" with a twist of "You're wrong!" sounds like.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 私にも理解できません。
English: Mr. Wright. I'm sorry, but I don't follow your logic.

Japanese: （まいったな。 たしかに、自信はない‥‥）
English: (Shoot. I guess I wasn't showing enough confidence...)

Japanese: どうですか？　弁護人。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥特に‥‥ありません。
English: Nothing, Your Honor...

Japanese: ‥‥認めるわけね。
English: That's very smart of you, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: この人物が、 綾里　真宵であることを。
English: You really should give up trying to prove this is not Maya Fey.

Japanese: （認めるわけじゃない）
English: (I didn't say anything about giving up!)

Japanese: （ただ、否定するだけの 証拠がないだけだ‥‥）
English: (I just don't have the right piece of evidence to prove my argument...)

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: （ここまで‥‥なのか‥‥？）
English: (I-Is this it...? Is this all I could do...?)

Japanese: 《完全無罪》を立証するのは、 やはりムリだったようですね。
English: I think it is quite obvious to this court that a verdict of not guilty is not possible.

Japanese: 言ったはずよ。 ゼッタイに不可能だ、と。
English: I told you. Totally, 100% impossible.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。‥‥この証人に、 まだ質問がありますか？
English: Ms. von Karma, do you have any further questions for this witness?

Japanese: 被告の犯行が立証された以上、 質問は意味を持たないわ。
English: Having established the defendant's guilt, further questioning won't be needed.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側も、 もう質問はないようですね。
English: What about the defense? No further questions, I presume?

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんを‥‥ 守ることができなかった‥‥） (I...
English: I couldn't protect Maya...)

Japanese: 質問は‥‥質問は‥‥
English: The defense... The defense...

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Phoenix...

Japanese: ‥弁護士が‥ ‥そんなカオしちゃダメ‥
English: ...Phoenix, you can't make that kind of face...

Japanese: ‥ピンチのときほど‥ ‥ふてぶてしく笑いなさい！‥
English: ...A lawyer is someone who smiles... no matter how bad it gets!...

Japanese: ち‥‥千尋さん！
English: M-Mia!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、 間に合ったみたいね‥‥。
English: I guess I made it just in time, wouldn't you say?

Japanese: ‥‥き、きみは‥‥ は、春美ちゃん！
English: Y-You... But... P-Pearls!

Japanese: ちょっと、ムネがキツいわ。
English: Her clothes ARE a bit small...

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。 一気に行きましょう！
English: Anyway, what are you waiting for, Phoenix!? Let's go!

Japanese: でも！　‥‥もう、 材料がありませんよ！
English: B-But how!? She's already taken away every advantage!

Japanese: 材料は、あなたの アタマの中に眠っているわ。
English: The advantage is still in your hand!

Japanese: もう一度、よく思い出して。 《対面の間》で見たことを‥‥。
English: Think carefully, one more time, about what you saw in the Channeling Chamber.

Japanese: きのう、私にも話して くれたでしょう？
English: Do you remember what you told me yesterday?

Japanese: う‥‥うわっ！　霧崎先生！
English: D-Dr. Grey!!
*klik*

Japanese: あたしは‥‥殺されたの‥‥
English: ...I was... murdered...

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって‥‥！
English: W-What!?
*klik*

Japanese: ナツミさん！ ‥‥こんなときに‥‥
English: Lotta! At a time like this!?

Japanese: こんなときだからこそ、や！
English: Times like this are perfect for snapping up shots!

Japanese: ‥‥ね。やっぱりまだ１つ、 手がかりがねむってる‥‥。
English: You see... There is one final piece of the puzzle.

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥？
English: Mia...?

Japanese: もう一度、ナツミさんに 尋問しましょう！
English: Question Lotta one more time, Phoenix.

Japanese: 無意識か故意かはわからない。 でも、彼女は‥‥、
English: I don't know if she's doing it subconsciously or on purpose, but...

Japanese: 証言していないことがあるわ！
English: she's not testifying truthfully.

Japanese: ‥‥では、この証人に対する 質問を終了します！
English: Now then, this court would like to end the cross- examination period.

Japanese: お待ちください裁判長！
English: Please wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: この証人にもう一度、 証言をおねがいします！
English: The defense would like to request that the witness testify one more time.

Japanese: 却下します。
English: Overruled.

Japanese: すでに、最後の尋問は 終了しました。
English: I'm afraid you're too late, Mr. Wright. The cross- examination has already ended.

Japanese: これ以上、ムダな質
English: Furthermore, any unrelated ques--

Japanese: はわっ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: いいでしょ。 ‥‥やらせてあげるわ。
English: It's fine. I'll allow another testimony.

Japanese: 狩魔の立証はカンペキ。 ギモンの余地はない‥‥。
English: A Von Karma's case is perfect. Absolutely flawless.

Japanese: そのことを思い知らせて あげる、いいチャンスよ。
English: And what better time than this for you to see that.

Japanese: い、いや、しかし。 私にも、このあとの予定が‥‥
English: B-But... I mean... I have some place I must go after this...

Japanese: まふっ！ ‥‥やや、やりますやります！
English: Mmph! A-Al-Alright! I'll allow it!

Japanese: 証人。早くしなさい。 ホラ、証言を！
English: Ms. Hart! Hurry and give us another testimony!

Japanese: そ‥‥そない言われても‥‥ 何をしゃべったらええんや？
English: Well... heck... What am I supposed to talk about?

Japanese: 《対面の間》に踏みこんだ時の ことを、もう一度おねがいします。
English: Please tell us once more what happened when you burst into the Channeling Chamber.

Japanese: おっけー。ええで！
English: OK! You got it!

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。なんとか つながったわね。‥‥審理。
English: See, you got through to the judge somehow, right?

Japanese: （もうダメかと思った‥‥）
English: (Well, actually it was Von Karma's whip that got through to him...)

Japanese: ～事件当日について・２～
English: -- Witness' Account, Pt.2 --

Japanese: あの部屋に入って、まず目に 入ったのは、真宵はんやったわ。
English: When we broke into that room, all I could focus on was Maya.

Japanese: 死体のほうは‥‥コワくてな。 ちゃんと見てないねん。
English: I was... uh, kinda scared of the dead body, so I didn't take a good look at it.

Japanese: 血ィとか、オバケとか、 ニガテやねんな、ウチ。
English: I'm really bad when it comes to blood and ghosts and stuff.

Japanese: で、カメラを真宵はんに向けて、 シャッターを押したワケや。
English: But I still managed to point my camera at Maya and take a shot!

Japanese: ‥‥先ほどの証言と、あまり 変わってないようですが‥‥。
English: Nothing sounds different from before...

Japanese: （この証言の中に、 ハッキリしない部分がある）
English: (Some parts of her testimony were very vague.)

Japanese: （そこを、ゆさぶるんだ！）
English: (I should press her on those sections!)

Japanese: あがけばあがくほど、 敗北の味はニガくなる。
English: Struggle all you like as you taste the bitterness of your defeat.

Japanese: さっさとやってもらおうかしら。 ムダな尋問を‥‥。
English: Well, amuse me with your useless questions, Mr. Phoenix Wright. I'm waiting...

Japanese: ～事件当日について・２～
English: -- Witness' Account, Pt.2 --

Japanese: あの部屋に入って、まず目に 入ったのは、真宵はんやったわ。
English: When we broke into that room, all I could focus on was Maya.

Japanese: 死体のほうは‥‥コワくてな。 ちゃんと見てないねん。
English: I was... uh, kinda scared of the dead body, so I didn't take a good look at it.

Japanese: 血ィとか、オバケとか、 ニガテやねんな、ウチ。
English: I'm really bad when it comes to blood and ghosts and stuff.

Japanese: で、カメラを真宵はんに向けて、 シャッターを押したワケや。
English: But I still managed to point my camera at Maya and take a shot!

Japanese: 今の証言の中にかならず、 手がかりがねむっているわ。
English: There must be something we can use in her testimony just now.

Japanese: 探し出して！ なんとしても‥‥。
English: We must find it! At all costs...

Japanese: 彼女のようすは どんな感じでしたか？
English: So, what did she look like?

Japanese: それ！　なんかなァ、 別人みたいやったワ。
English: Ya know, she looked almost like a different person.

Japanese: 別人‥‥ですか？
English: A... A different person?

Japanese: でもその人物は、 綾里　真宵だった。
English: She may have seemed like a different person, however, she was still Maya Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥さっきから何度も 証明されてるコトよ！
English: We have already made that point abundantly clear!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりですな。
English: Yes, that is true...

Japanese: それで、証人。 他に目に入ったものは？
English: Well, then, Ms. Hart. Did you happen to see anything else?

Japanese: じゃあその死体は、霧崎医師では なかったかもしれませんね！
English: Then perhaps that body was not Dr. Grey's at all!

Japanese: ええッ！　‥‥ま、まあ‥‥ そうかもしれへんケドなぁ。
English: Eh!? W-Well... I reckon that's possible...

Japanese: 部屋の中にころがってたのが 霧崎　哲郎でないとしたら、
English: If the body inside that room was not that of Dr. Grey,

Japanese: いったいダレだったと 言うのッ！
English: then whose body was it, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: ‥‥たとえば、その死体のほうが 霊媒した真宵ちゃんだった、とか。
English: Well, what if it was that of Maya Fey after the channeling? Or maybe --

Japanese: いてっ！
English: Oww!

Japanese: ‥‥バカがバカにふさわしく バカなことを言ってるようね。
English: Foolish fool spouting foolish foolishness, just as I expect of a foolish fool such as you.

Japanese: もしそうなら、綾里　真宵は 死んでいなければならない。
English: If that were the case, then that would mean that Maya Fey is, in fact, dead.

Japanese: あなたの依頼人なら、被告席で しょんぼりしてるでしょうが！
English: And that would make the defendant's chair quite a lonely place, wouldn't it!?

Japanese: （‥‥ゴメン、真宵ちゃん）
English: (Urk... Sorry, Maya...)

Japanese: ええかぁ？　つづけても？
English: Are y'all done here? Can I continue?

Japanese: それなのに、オカルト カメラマンに？
English: And even knowing that, you still persist in being a paranormal photographer?

Japanese: だから、もうヤメヤメ。
English: That's why I'm getting out of this here business.

Japanese: 今日からウチは、ゴシップ専門の イヤらしい芸能カメラマンや！
English: This gal's gonna get the dirt on the stars and be a tabloid photographer from now on!

Japanese: （イバってるよ‥‥）
English: (She goes through jobs faster than a baby through diapers.)

Japanese: うわっ！
English: Wah!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 事件に関係のある質問に しぼってもらえるかしら？
English: Would it kill you to stay on topic for a change?

Japanese: しぼってもらえませんか？ 弁護人。
English: Well? Would it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （‥‥いたいよお‥‥）
English: (Nice, tag-teamed in stereo. Guess I should be grateful it's not in 5.1...)

Japanese: ２回‥‥ですね？
English: Two, right?

Japanese: な‥‥ナニがや？
English: T-Two what?

Japanese: シャッターですよ。
English: Two shots.

Japanese: あなたは２回、 シャッターを押した。
English: You took two shots.

Japanese: しかし‥‥提出されているのは、 この１枚きりですぞ！
English: But... Only one has been submitted as evidence!

Japanese: ナツミさん！　あなたは、 写真を１枚、かくしている！
English: Ms. Hart! Where is the other picture you have been withholding!?

Japanese: い、いやいやいや。 ウチやないで！
English: No, no, no! You've got it all wrong! It's not me!

Japanese: わわ、悪気があったワケやない！ そこまでヒドい女ちゃうよウチは！
English: I-I didn't mean anything bad! I know I can be a little mean, but I ain't evil!

Japanese: （多少はヒドい女であると わかってるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Well, at least she knows herself...)

Japanese: では、なぜ 提出しなかったんですか？
English: Then why have you not shown this "other picture" to this court yet?

Japanese: 決まってるやん！ そこの検事さんに止められたんや。
English: Well, ain't it obvious!? That prosecutor woman told me to shut my trap about it!

Japanese: なんだって！
English: W-What!?

Japanese: 狩魔検事ィィッ！
English: Von Karma!!

Japanese: かかか、狩魔検事！ あ、あなたは‥‥重要な証拠を！
English: M-M-Ms. von Karma! Y-Y-You... You're hiding critical evidence...!

Japanese: （ゆ‥‥ユルせない、狩魔め！）
English: (Unforgivable, Von Karma.)

Japanese: （証拠をインメツしようと したんだ‥‥！）
English: (Suppressing evidence like this... You're as bad as your father!)

Japanese: 裁判長！ 弁護側は、狩魔　冥を訴えます！
English: Your Honor! The defense asks that Prosecutor von Karma be held in contempt of court!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうですね‥‥
English: Hmm... Well, yes...

Japanese: めうっ！
English: Mngh!

Japanese: 私を訴える‥‥？
English: Me? In contempt of court!?

Japanese: そのコンキョを 聞かせてくれるかしら。
English: You can't be serious.

Japanese: だって！　証拠をかくしたんだぞ！ それは、その‥‥！
English: But... But you hid evidence from the court... On purpose! That's... That's...

Japanese: イケないんだぞ！
English: That's not fair!

Japanese: なな、なるほどくん！ もうちょっと、弁護士っぽく！
English: P-Phoenix!! Are you a lawyer or a school child?

Japanese: （しまった！　コーフンのあまり ダダっ子になっちまった‥‥）
English: (Ack! I was so angry I lost my composure...)

Japanese: （‥‥ぐ。怒りがノドにつまって コトバにならない！）
English: (...Argh! I'm so mad that even my throat's clenching up!)

Japanese: （ここは、 裁判長にまかせよう‥‥）
English: (I'd better let the Judge handle this...)

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事。 セツメイをおねがいしましょうか。
English: Ms. von Karma. Would you care to explain yourself to this court?

Japanese: こんな写真、提出する必要なし。 ‥‥そう思っただけよ。
English: I thought this picture was not important, and thus, felt no need to submit it. That's all.

Japanese: なんだと‥‥！
English: H-How could you think...!?

Japanese: ２枚の写真は、ほぼ同じ内容が 写っていたの。
English: The pictures were more or less the same, so why bother with the second?

Japanese: ‥‥ただ、こちらを提出すると、 メンドウになりそうだったから。
English: I mean, it's just so much more trouble than it's worth for me to submit this as evidence.

Japanese: め、メンドウだって‥‥！ そんな‥‥
English: "M-More trouble that it's worth!?" How... How can you say that!?

Japanese: そんなに見たけりゃ、 提出してあげるわ。
English: If you want to see it that badly, I'll submit it.

Japanese: 証拠品《ナツミの写真・２》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Lotta's Photo 2 added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なんですか、この写真は‥‥。
English: Wh-What is...? What is this picture!?

Japanese: 証人が現場で撮影した、 ２枚目の写真に決まってるわ。
English: It's obviously the second picture the witness took that day at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: ビビってカメラがブレたみたいね。 ‥‥なさけないカメラマン。
English: Well, it's a little off center. But that's what you get with a nervous amateur.

Japanese: そんなコトより‥‥ ここに写っているのは、
English: Well, I think the important point here is the person in this picture!

Japanese: あきらかに、 綾里　真宵ではない！
English: This is most definitely NOT Maya Fey!

Japanese: いったいこれは、 どういうことですか！
English: What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: （‥‥こいつは、 チャンスかもしれない‥‥）
English: (This might be the break I was looking for...)

Japanese: （この写真の人物が“真宵ちゃん じゃない”と主張するか？）
English: (Now, should I or should I not insist that the person in this picture is not Maya?)

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 見てのとおり、これは 綾里　真宵ではありません！
English: As you can plainly see, the person in the picture is not Maya Fey!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですね‥‥
English: Y-Yes, I see that...

Japanese: カオつきばかりか、体型まで ちがうように見えます！
English: It's not just her face! Her whole body has taken on a different appearance!

Japanese: 考えられることは１つ。
English: There is only one conclusion that can be drawn, then.

Japanese: どこかで、被告と入れ替わった 人間が存在するのです！
English: That somehow, this other person snuck in and traded places with the defendant!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ そういうことになります！
English: Well, yes, I believe you are correct, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 《対面の間》にいたのは、 綾里　真宵ではなかった！
English: The person who was inside the Channeling Chamber was not Maya Fey!

Japanese: 千尋さん！　やりましたよ！
English: Mia! We did it!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥アマいわ。
English: ...You're too soft.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: キミ子おばさまの大福より アマいわね、なるほどくん。
English: You're softer than Aunt Morgan's strawberry desserts, Phoenix.

Japanese: 狩魔検事のカオをごらんなさい。
English: Take a look at Prosecutor von Karma's face.

Japanese: （や、やれやれ‥‥笑ってるよ）
English: (S-She's... smiling...!?)

Japanese: シバイはヘタみたいね。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: I think you had better continue to worry, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか、 狩魔検事。
English: M-Ms. von Karma! What do you mean by that?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥。 （ムダだ。やめておこう）
English: (It's no good. We've been through this so many times...)

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしました？ なぜ異議を申し立てないのです！
English: This is not like you, Mr. Wright! Why are you not objecting!?

Japanese: この写真の人物は、アキラカに 被告人ではない‥‥
English: The person in this photo is clearly not the defendant and yet...

Japanese: ふっふっふっ‥‥。
English: Ha ha ha...

Japanese: この弁護人に、異議なんか 申し立てられるハズないわ。
English: There is no way he can raise an objection and hope to live.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしてですかッ！
English: Explain yourself!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。 この写真をごらんなさい。
English: Your Honor. I would like for you to take a look at this picture.

Japanese: これはきのう、留置所の 面会室で撮影したものよ。
English: This was taken yesterday at the Detention Center's Visitor's Room.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一と、綾里　真宵の 面会中に‥‥。
English: This is Mr. Phoenix Wright and the defendant, Maya Fey during his visit...

Japanese: 綾里　真宵？
English: Maya Fey?

Japanese: しかしこれは‥‥ ゼンゼン別人ではないですか！
English: But... Isn't this a totally different person!?

Japanese: アタリマエよ。なにしろ‥‥ 彼女は霊媒中だったのだから！
English: She looks this way because she is obviously in the middle of channeling a spirit!

Japanese: し‥‥信じられません‥‥ こ、このような‥‥
English: It's unbelievable... That she can...

Japanese: しかし、ジジツよ。
English: However, it is true.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵は、霊媒で 別人にすがたを変えてしまう！
English: When Maya Fey is in that state, she physically changes into the person she's calling!

Japanese: ちょっと‥‥ いいかしら？　検事さん。
English: I wonder if she knows what she's doing...

Japanese: （ち、千尋さん！）
English: (M-Mia!)

Japanese: 面会中の写真を撮影するのは 違法のはず。
English: Taking pictures of someone during a private visit is illegal.

Japanese: その写真は、証拠には なりませんよ。
English: That picture can't be submitted as evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥あたりまえでしょ。 こんなの違法よ。
English: Obviously, this picture is illegal.

Japanese: 証拠として提出する気なんて、 あるワケないじゃない。
English: But I never intended to submit it as evidence in the first place!

Japanese: （な、なんだって‥‥）
English: (Wh-What is she...?)

Japanese: でもね。見せちゃえば、 こっちの勝ち。
English: From the moment I showed this picture to the court, this case became all mine.

Japanese: もう、裁判長のアタマから、 この映像を消すことはできない。
English: After all, this image has now been forever burned into the judge's mind.

Japanese: （カンゼンに放心してるぞ‥‥）
English: (I... I don't have even a single witty line...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥やられたわね。
English: It looks like we've been had.

Japanese: どうしても信じられませんが、 やはり‥‥
English: I don't claim to understand this, and I still cannot believe it...

Japanese: この人物は‥‥被告人、 綾里　真宵ということですか！
English: Are you saying that this person... this person is the defendant, Maya Fey!?

Japanese: （それを認めてしまえば、 審理は終了してしまう！）
English: (If I give up here, the trial will end!)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥ここでイイカゲンな 発言はイノチ取りだ‥‥）
English: (But... If I slip up and say something wrong, it will cost me...)

Japanese: （これが真宵ちゃんじゃない 可能性を立証できるか‥‥？）
English: (Is there any way I can prove that the person in this picture is not Maya!?)

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ！　どう考えても、 立証できるわけがない！）
English: (It's no good! My argument doesn't hold any water!)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、弁護側にも 異議はないようですね。
English: I see the defense is staying silent on this one.
...Yes... Your Honor...

Japanese: 私は弁護側に、あらゆる 反論のチャンスを与えた。
English: And to think. I even gave you this chance to prove your point for free.

Japanese: でも‥‥もうタネ切れみたいね。
English: But you couldn't even point yourself out in a line-up!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうですね。
English: Hmm... Yes...

Japanese: わかりました。 そろそろ、終わりにしましょう。
English: Well, then. I believe we have reached a conclusion.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Phoenix!

Japanese: は‥‥はい。
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: おねがい、あきらめないで！
English: You can't give up! Please, for me?

Japanese: 法廷記録を見て‥‥ なんとか、逃げ道を探すの！
English: Take a look at the Court Record again and try to find some way out of this jam!

Japanese: “なんとか”、って‥‥
English: Yeah... Some way...

Japanese: とにかく、 審理を終了させてはダメよ！
English: The judge is about to close the trial. You can't let that happen!

Japanese: （くそ‥‥もう どうにでもなれ！）
English: (Argh... It's come down to this!)

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: この写真‥‥これまでの証言と、 大きくムジュンしています！
English: Within this picture lies a critical contradiction to all the testimony up until now.

Japanese: なな、なんですか今ごろ！
English: What is it, now, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: おねがいです！ 言わせてください！
English: Please! Please let me explain!

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥お。目にカガヤキが もどりましたね‥‥。
English: Oh! The fire has returned to your eyes, I see.

Japanese: この写真‥‥。
English: This picture...

Japanese: これまでの証言と、 大きくムジュンしています！
English: Within this picture lies a critical contradiction to all the testimony up until now.

Japanese: “ムジュン”‥‥ですって！
English: A contradiction!?

Japanese: ‥‥見苦しい時間かせぎ。 写真にモンクをつける気？
English: So, you think you've spotted a problem with this picture? Then earn your keep.

Japanese: いったい、この写真のどこが オカシイと言うの？
English: Why don't you point out exactly what is so strange in this picture for the court?

Japanese: ここを見てください！
English: Please direct your attention here!

Japanese: ‥‥被告のカオ‥‥ですか？
English: ...T-To her... face?

Japanese: このカオ、どう見たって 綾里　真宵ではありません！
English: With your own two eyes, you can clearly see that this face does not belong to Maya Fey!
Fool!

Japanese: 今まで、ナニを聞いてたのよ！
English: Do you even know what you're talking about anymore!?

Japanese: うわわ！
English: AAAH!

Japanese: いて！
English: THAT HURT!

Japanese: 霊媒中にカオが変わるコトは とっくにわかってるでしょ！
English: Didn't we just finish discussing that her appearance changes during channeling!?

Japanese: アンタ、トボけんのも いいかげんにせぇや！
English: I know you ain't no dummy, so don't think yer foolin' no one here!

Japanese: ものごとには ゲンドがありますぞ！
English: There is a limit to everything, Mr. Wright. And that includes patience!

Japanese: もう！　ちゃんと 考えなきゃダメでしょ！
English: Phoenix! You have to think before you speak! You can't just blurt out any old thing!

Japanese: ‥‥見そこなったよっ、 なるほどくん！
English: ...Nick! You're supposed to be helping me!

Japanese: （うう‥‥被告席の 真宵ちゃんまで‥‥）
English: (*cringe* Even Maya's mad at me now... I have to focus!)

Japanese: ムジュンしているのは‥‥ ここらへんです！
English: The contradiction lies... Here!

Japanese: ‥‥ココロなしか、 自信なさそうですね。
English: ...You don't sound very sure of yourself, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ええ！　自信なんて あるもんですか！
English: Excuse me, but can you really tell me how much confidence I have in this!?
Hmph!

Japanese: ナニよそのタイドはッ！
English: What is with that attitude!?

Japanese: ここを見てください。
English: Please direct your attention here!

Japanese: 被告のたもと‥‥ですか？
English: ...T-To the sleeve?

Japanese: ‥‥ベツに、おかしな ところはありませんが‥‥。
English: But... But there isn't anything odd about it...

Japanese: それがおかしいんですよ。
English: And that is exactly what is so odd.

Japanese: 当然あるべきものが、 ここにはない。
English: Something that should be there is suddenly missing.

Japanese: “あるべきもの”‥‥？
English: "Should be there"...?

Japanese: あああっ！
English: Aaaaah!

Japanese: 被告の装束のたもとには、 弾痕がありました！
English: There was a bullet hole in the sleeve of the defendant's costume!

Japanese: それならば、当然！ 写真にも写っているはずです！
English: If that's the case, then it should be in this picture as well!

Japanese: 狩魔検事！　あなたは‥‥
English: Ms. von Karma!! Y-You...

Japanese: こんな大事な証拠品を かくそうとしたのですかッ！
English: You intended to hide this valuable piece of evidence!?

Japanese: あなたには‥‥しかるべき ペナルティを与えます！
English: You will most certainly be assigned a penalty for this!

Japanese: （よし‥‥これでヤツに 大きなダメージを‥‥）
English: (Alright. This should do some major damage to her argument...)

Japanese: ‥‥アマいわ。
English: Don't celebrate yet.

Japanese: まま、またですか？
English: You like to bring down the mood, don't you?

Japanese: 狩魔検事のカオをごらんなさい。
English: Take a look at Ms. von Karma's face.

Japanese: （お、おいおい‥‥笑ってるよ）
English: (Argh! She's got that condescending grin plastered all over her face again.)
...Tsk, tsk, jumping the gun again, I see. Your Honor!

Japanese: オロカな警察にかわって、 私のほうからおわびするわ。
English: I would like to extend an apology on behalf of those incompetent fools.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか。 ‥‥ケイサツ？
English: Wh-What do you mean? And what "incompetent fools"...?

Japanese: そもそも、この装束の弾痕を 見落としたのは、彼らよ。
English: If those fools down at the precinct hadn't missed the bullet hole,

Japanese: 私は当然、弾痕のことなど 報告されていない。
English: I would have gotten a report about it.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、この写真が 重要なものと知りようがなかった。
English: As I didn't, I could not have known that this picture was of any value to this case.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ そうだったのですか‥‥。
English: Hmm, I see...

Japanese: （‥‥そんなハズはない！）
English: (She's lying through her teeth! I know it!)

Japanese: （あの女は気づいていたんだ！ 装束の弾痕も‥‥写真も！）
English: (That woman knew about everything! The bullet hole, the picture -- everything!)

Japanese: ‥‥でも、 それは立証できない。
English: But you can't prove that.

Japanese: 狩魔の“カンペキ主義”‥‥。 なかなかおもしろいわね。
English: Franziska von Karma's idea of a "perfect case" is quite fascinating, don't you think?

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥シンパイはいらない。
English: Your Honor. You need not worry.

Japanese: ペナルティなら、あの刑事に イヤというほど与えておくから。
English: If you must assign a penalty, I'll personally make sure that detective gets what's coming.

Japanese: 次回の給料査定で、ものすごい ドラマが起きるハズよ。
English: I'm sure there will be a great gnashing of teeth at the next salary discussion.

Japanese: （カワイソウなイトノコ刑事‥‥）
English: (Poor Gumshoe...)

Japanese: とにかく、これは 大きなムジュンです！
English: In any case, this is a very big problem!

Japanese: 逮捕されたとき、被告人の 装束には弾痕がありました。
English: When the defendant was taken into custody, her costume had a bullet hole in its sleeve.

Japanese: しかし‥‥発砲直後には、 それがない！
English: However, from this photo, it would appear that right after the shooting, there was none!

Japanese: 今‥‥裁判長のココロが ゆらいでいるわ。
English: The judge is confused by this strange twist of events.

Japanese: チャンスよ、なるほどくん。 ‥‥一気に攻撃しましょう！
English: This is your chance, Phoenix! Load all you've got into this one shot, alright!?

Japanese: はい！ （‥‥真宵ちゃん、見ててくれ！）
English: Got it! (Watch this, Maya!)

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: この写真が意味するのは ただ１つ！
English: There is only one logical explanation for the contradiction!

Japanese: 被告の装束に弾痕が あったのは、ジジツです！
English: The fact that the defendant's costume has a bullet hole in its sleeve is a fact!

Japanese: ということは、答えは１つ。
English: Which can only mean one thing.

Japanese: ‥‥その写真は、インチキです！
English: That this picture is a fake!

Japanese: なんですって！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: アンタッ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: ウチがなんや、 インチキした言うんかい！
English: You callin' me a forger now!?

Japanese: ど、どうでしょうか‥‥。
English: Well, no, not you exactly... Well, not per se...

Japanese: コラァ！ ビミョーに目ェそらすなや！
English: Oh no ya don't! Don't you be scratchin' yer head with that goofy smile like some monkey!

Japanese: なんでウチがそんな ヒドいコトせなアカンねん！
English: Now, why do ya think I'd do somethin' that nasty!? C'mon now!

Japanese: ホラ！　考えなおして！
English: Phoenix! Use your brain for a change!

Japanese: す‥‥すみません‥‥。
English: S-Sorry, Chief...

Japanese: 写真で、たもとに穴がない‥‥。 ならば、答えは１つしかない！
English: There is only one explanation for why there is no hole in the sleeve in the picture!

Japanese: たもとに穴があいたのは、 もっと後だったのです！
English: The hole was created much later, after the murder took place!

Japanese: そ、そうなのですか！
English: A-After the murder!?

Japanese: アンタッ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: ウチらが２発、銃声を聞いたのは 現場に踏みこむ“前”やないかい！
English: Those two gunshots we heard were before we broke in, or did ya forget!?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうでしたっけ。
English: Hmm... I-Is that how it went?

Japanese: ピストルの弾丸は、 ２発しか撃たれていない。
English: The pistol fired only two shots. No more, no less.

Japanese: 弾丸の数が合わなくなって しまうじゃないですか！
English: You do know how to count, right?

Japanese: 考えなおして、なるほどくん！
English: Phoenix! What are you doing!? Try it one more time!

Japanese: は‥‥はい。
English: Y-Yes, Chief...

Japanese: 被告のたもとには、 このとおり弾痕があります。
English: The defendant's sleeve had a bullet hole in it.

Japanese: しかし、この人物のたもとには、 それがない。
English: However, this person clearly does not.

Japanese: ‥‥その理由は１つ。
English: There can be only one explanation.

Japanese: ピストルを撃った人物は、 被告ではなかったのです！
English: The person who shot Dr. Grey was not the defendant, but a different person altogether!

Japanese: なんですってえェェ！
English: Wha-Whaaaat!?

Japanese: ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: 静まらなければ、 審理を中断しますぞ！
English: If order is not restored, I will suspend this trial!

Japanese: ひひいッ！
English: Oww!

Japanese: あちっ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: ウチもかいっ！
English: Why me!?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人の主張は、 もうメチャクチャよッ！
English: The defense's... The defense's argument is a complete mess!

Japanese: どのへんがメチャクチャ なのか、教えてください！
English: A complete mess? I fail to see how! Please, enlighten us.

Japanese: コラッ、証人！
English: Hey, witness!

Japanese: ぎゃあっ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: そこでウチに来るんか‥‥。 な、なんやッ！
English: What the heck!? Is that any way to ask a gal a favor!?

Japanese: あなた言ったわよね！
English: Be quiet you!

Japanese: 現場に踏みこんだとき、 被告と被害者しかいなかったって！
English: You were the one who said it was only the two of them when you entered the room!

Japanese: ま、まあなぁ‥‥。
English: Well, ya know...

Japanese: ウソだったら、ひっぱたく！
English: If you were lying, I swear that my whip will be the last thing you see!

Japanese: ね‥‥ネエちゃん、 アンタちょっとコワいで‥‥。
English: L-Look, sis... You're lookin' mighty scary so why don't we say you...

Japanese: ひゃあっ！
English: Waah!

Japanese: まま‥‥マチガイないでェッ！
English: I-I swear! I wasn't lyin' or nothin'!

Japanese: 部屋の中には、 他にダレもいなかったハズやッ！
English: There wasn't anyone else in there! Honest!

Japanese: ほら見なさい！ 答えなさいよ成歩堂　龍一！
English: You see! Now riddle me this, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: 被告はどこへ消えたの！
English: Where did the defendant vanish to!?

Japanese: それに、この写真の女は どこから入ってきたのッ！
English: And where did this other woman appear from!?

Japanese: ‥‥うう。 （最近、ナミダもろくなったな）
English: Umm... (Why is it lately, all I want to do is cry...?)

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、この人物が 被告でないとなると‥‥、
English: Well, if the person in this picture is not the defendant,

Japanese: ２つのギモンが生まれますね。
English: then this poses 2 very big questions.

Japanese: 被告はどこへ消えたのか？ この人物はどこから来たのか？
English: First, where did the defendant vanish to, and second, where did this person come from?

Japanese: さあさあさあ！ 答えなさいよ成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: That's right! Now hurry up and answer, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: あとには引けないわ！ がんばるの！　成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: Come on! You can't fall apart here, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: （‥‥千尋さんまで フルネームで‥‥）
English: (I can't believe that even Mia's calling me by my full name...)

Japanese: （でも‥‥そんなコト‥‥ 証明できるわけがない！）
English: (But... I mean... How am I supposed to prove something like this!?)

Japanese: いかがかしら？　成歩堂　龍一。
English: Had enough yet, Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: 最後の最後、 逆転される気分は！
English: Or do you think you have enough in you to turn things around even now!?
(To "turn things around"...)

Japanese: （そうだ。千尋さんはピンチのとき いつも、発想を逆転させていた！）
English: (That's right. Mia would always turn things around and change her perspective!)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥？
English: Phoenix...?

Japanese: （犯人はどこから現場に来たのか？ 真宵ちゃんはどこへ消えたか？）
English: (So, where did this intruder appear from, and where did Maya disappear to?)

Japanese: （‥‥それが証明できないなら、 逆転させて考えるんだ！）
English: (I need to look at this situation from a different angle!)

Japanese: （つまり‥‥）
English: (Let's see...)

Japanese: （ぼくたちが現場に踏みこむまでに 第３者があの部屋に入った‥‥）
English: (What if before we broke in, the third person was already in the room...)

Japanese: （もしくは、真宵ちゃんが あの部屋から出た‥‥）
English: (And what if Maya had left that room somehow...)

Japanese: （そのどちらかが起こったはずだ。 ‥‥それを証明できればいい！）
English: (If I could prove that either one of those conditions were true...)

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥あなたの 考えを聞かせてください。
English: Mr. Wright. Let's hear what you've come up with.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくたちが現場に踏みこむ までに、《対面の間》では‥‥
English: I think what happened before we forced our way into the Channeling Chamber is...

Japanese: 今、ここで答えを 提示することは‥‥できません。
English: I'm sorry, but I simply can't.

Japanese: ‥‥ほ、ホラごらんなさい！
English: Y-You see!?

Japanese: その理由はカンタン。 答えなど、存在しないからよ！
English: And the reason is quite simple. Because there is no explanation. That's why!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 やはり、時間のムダでしたか。
English: Hmm... As I thought. Another big waste of this court's time.

Japanese: ダメ！　なるほどくん！ それだけは認めちゃいけない！
English: Phoenix! You can't just give up like that!

Japanese: ち、千尋さん‥‥！
English: But Mia!

Japanese: もう一度、しっかり考えて！ 何か、証明する方法があるはず！
English: Rethink things one more time! There has to be an explanation!

Japanese: 私は“カンペキ”なのよ。 ‥‥その上を行こうと言うの‥‥？
English: I'm already "perfect". So of course, my conclusions are also "perfect".

Japanese: （くそ‥‥落ちついて、 もう一度、整理してみるんだ！）
English: (Grr... Alright, Wright. Calm down and let's try this one more time!)

Japanese: 裁判長！　ここはやはり、 証拠品を見てもらいましょう！
English: Your Honor! I would like to present some evidence!

Japanese: 事件が起こるまでに、現場に 第３者が入りこんでいた証拠を！
English: I have proof that there was someone else in the room before the murder took place!

Japanese: ‥‥これを見てください！
English: Please take a good look at this!

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: ...Okay...

Japanese: 見たわよ。
English: I've already seen it.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: ‥‥で。 これがどうかしましたか。
English: And? What is the purpose of this item?

Japanese: おわかりになりませんか。
English: Don't you see, Your Honor?

Japanese: あなたは？ おわかりになっているの？
English: I don't think you actually see what you are doing.
*sigh*

Japanese: タメイキはないでしょう！ 考えなおしてください。
English: I think it is the court that should be sighing at you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （とほほ‥‥）
English: (Ouch, that stung...)

Japanese: （どうも、こっちの考え方では、 証明はムズカシそうだな）
English: (I think the way I'm going about this is all wrong...)

Japanese: （最初から考えなおすか‥‥）
English: (I have to start over and try to think about things in a different manner...)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは《対面の間》から、 そとに出たのです！
English: Maya had left the Channeling Chamber at some point!

Japanese: 弁護側は、 それを証明することができる！
English: And the defense can prove this!

Japanese: ‥‥バカがバカなカオして バカなユメを見てるようね。
English: Oh, how a foolish fool makes a foolish face while dreaming foolishly foolish dreams.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵は、おばのキミ子に 介抱されていたのよ。
English: Maya Fey was being looked after by her aunt, Morgan Fey.

Japanese: 現場から出られるはずがない！
English: The chances of her leaving the crime scene is lower than that Detective's salary!

Japanese: とにかく、証拠を 見せてもらいましょう。
English: Anyway, let's see some evidence.

Japanese: 事件発生から逮捕までのあいだ、 被告が部屋のそとに出た証拠を！
English: Prove that from the murder until the time of arrest, the defendant had left the room.

Japanese: その証拠は‥‥これです？
English: The evidence you're looking for is... this?

Japanese: ‥‥なんで最後が “？”なんや！
English: Why the heck is there a "?" at the end there for!?

Japanese: いや。さすがにぼくも、 そこまで自信はないですから。
English: Well, actually, I'm not really sure about this evidence, you know...?

Japanese: 自信のある証拠だけ、 見せてください。
English: Next time, only present evidence you actually do have faith in.

Japanese: （とほほ‥‥）
English: (Oww... That burned...)

Japanese: ナツミさん。‥‥このカギ、 おぼえてますか？
English: Ms. Hart. Do you remember this key?

Japanese: たしかそれ‥‥、 《対面の間》のカギやろ？
English: Um... Well, I've seen it... Hey! That's the Channeling Chamber key, right?

Japanese: 霊媒が始まる前に、真宵はんが 部屋の中からカギをかけた‥‥。
English: Before the channeling started, Maya locked the door from the inside with that...

Japanese: 被告が、カギを‥‥？
English: The defendant herself locked the door...?

Japanese: そうや。だからウチら、 扉をコワしたんや。
English: Yeah! That's why we all couldn't get the door open.

Japanese: ‥‥なんせ、あの扉のカギは、 １つしかなかったからナ。
English: That key's the only one of its kind, after all.

Japanese: ほほお‥‥ １つしかなかった‥‥。
English: Oh... One of a kind, you say?

Japanese: ちょっと待って ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Wait... Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: なんですか？ （ウスウスわかってきたらしいな）
English: Yes? (It looks like she's catching on...)

Japanese: 聞くのがコワいけど‥‥。
English: I'm afraid to ask, but...

Japanese: どうしてそのカギを、 今、あなたが持ってるの？
English: Why is that key currently in your possession?

Japanese: え。‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Huh? What do you mean?

Japanese: 綾里　真宵がカギをかけたのなら、 カギは彼女が持っているはずです。
English: If Maya Fey locked herself in, then the key should have been with her.

Japanese: そのとおりです。
English: Yes, agreed.

Japanese: しかし逮捕されたとき、彼女は カギなど持っていなかった！
English: However, she did not have the key at the time of her arrest!

Japanese: ああっ！　そ‥‥そうや！
English: Aah! Well, ain't that a kick!

Japanese: なんでそれ、 アンタが持ってるんやッ！
English: So how come you're holdin' it!?

Japanese: このカギは、ある女の子から プレゼントされました。
English: I got this as a present from a certain little girl.

Japanese: その子は現場に、 近づいてすらいません！
English: And that little girl was nowhere near the crime scene at the time!

Japanese: そそ‥‥そ、そんな‥‥ あり得ない‥‥！
English: Th-Th-That's preposterous!

Japanese: つまり！　綾里　真宵は、 あの部屋から出ていたことになる！
English: This means that Maya Fey must have left the room!

Japanese: そうでなければ、ぼくが カギを持っているはずがない！
English: If she had not, then I would not be holding this key you see before you!

Japanese: バ、バカなああぁぁァッ！
English: N-Nooooooo!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやらワレワレは、 大きな問題にぶつかったようです。
English: It seems we have come to an impasse.

Japanese: 犯人は、ハッキリしています。 このとおり、写真に写っている。
English: This picture has clearly captured the face of the murderer.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。この人物は、 はたして被告人だったのか？
English: However, is this person the defendant or not?

Japanese: 弁護側は、被告人ではなかったと 主張しています。
English: The defense is arguing that this person is not the defendant.

Japanese: そして、その証拠として、 このカギを提出しました。
English: Furthermore, as proof, this key has been submitted as evidence.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。
English: Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: ‥‥バカな‥‥
English: How. Can. This. Be!?

Japanese: 今の時点で、被告に判決を 下すことはできません。
English: At this point in time, a verdict on the defendant is not possible.

Japanese: 私のカンペキな立証に ケチをつけるなんて‥‥
English: My perfect case! How is there a flaw in my perfect case!

Japanese: これで勝ったと思わないことね。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: Don't think you've won yet, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: 私はアメリカで無敗を ほこった天才なのよ。
English: I am a prodigy! I have never lost a case!

Japanese: ‥‥それに。この法廷だけは、 負けるわけにはいかない！
English: And I don't intend to lose here in this courtroom to a fool like you!

Japanese: どんな手を使っても‥‥ 有罪判決をいただくわ！
English: I don't care what I have to do... I will get my guilty verdict!

Japanese: そこまで！　つづきは、 控え室でおねがいします。
English: That's enough! If you would like to continue, do so in the lobby.

Japanese: 次回の審理は、明日１０時。 ‥‥よろしいですね。
English: Court will reconvene tomorrow at 10 AM.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、 本日は、これにて閉廷！
English: That is all. Court is adjourned.

Japanese: 同日　午後　１時３２分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: June 21, 1:32 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: えーっ！ あれ、はみちゃんだったの！
English: Woooow! That was you Pearly!? You summoned my sis!?

Japanese: はい。ああするしかない、 って思って‥‥。
English: Yes. I felt I had no choice...

Japanese: さっすが、はみちゃんだよねー。 なるほどくん、気づいた？
English: Great going, Pearly! I knew you were special! Hey, Nick, did you know?

Japanese: （‥‥他にダレがいるって 言うんだよ‥‥）
English: (...Umm, yeah. It's not like anyone else in there could have done that.)

Japanese: でもさ。なるほどくん。
English: Hmm, Nick...

Japanese: せっかく助けてくれたのに アレなんだけど‥‥。
English: I know you're trying really hard and all, but...

Japanese: あたし‥‥あの部屋から 出たおぼえ、ないんだよねー。
English: I really don't remember ever leaving that room.

Japanese: それに、第３者があの部屋に 入ったというのも、
English: And I don't think that a third person...

Japanese: かなりムリがあると思います。
English: could have gone into that room.

Japanese: た‥‥たしかになあ。
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: とにかく、明日までには 見つけてみせるよ。
English: Well, at least we have until tomorrow to figure things out.

Japanese: あの部屋で何があったか？ ‥‥その答えを、ね。
English: Like, what happened in that room, for instance.

Japanese: うん。たのんだ！
English: Yeah. I'm counting on you!

Japanese: はあ‥‥。 うらやましいです‥‥。
English: Ah... I envy the two of you...

Japanese: あ。そうだ。
English: Oh, by the way, Nick.

Japanese: なるほどくん。はみちゃんを 送ってあげてね。
English: Do you think you could take Pearly back home for me?

Japanese: あ。うん。
English: Sure.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、 キップ、買いに行こうか。
English: Alright, Pearls. You ready to go buy some tickets?

Japanese: はい？ “きっぷ”‥‥ですか？
English: Huh? A tik-ket?

Japanese: （やっぱり、ダメだこりゃ）
English: (Poor thing... So sheltered.)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、綾里　真宵に この場で、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Maya Fey...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
